roofing.pain: crews on roofs can't answer; storm calls hit at 2am
roofing.hook: In roofing a single missed call is worth about $2,800 — and roof jobs run eight to twenty-five grand. Miss one storm call and that's a competitor's week made.

hvac.pain: emergency calls spike when every tech is already dispatched
hvac.hook: An HVAC missed call averages about $1,500, and replacements run three to ten grand. When the AC dies in July, whoever answers first wins.

plumbing.pain: burst-pipe callers don't shop around, they call the next name
plumbing.hook: A missed plumbing call runs about $1,200. Someone with a flooded basement isn't waiting for a callback — they're dialing the next plumber.

electrical.pain: techs can't stop mid-job to answer
electrical.hook: Each missed call costs an electrician around $950 — and that's before the repeat work and referrals you never hear about.

legal.pain: first firm to respond usually wins the case; 42% take 3+ days
legal.hook: In law a missed intake is three to five grand minimum — personal injury cases run far higher. The firm that answers first usually gets the case.

realestate.pain: speed-to-lead is everything; 78% go with whoever answers first
realestate.hook: A missed buyer call on a $300k home is around $9,000 in commission. 78% of callers just go with the first agent who actually picks up.

auto.pain: front desk busy with in-bay customers
auto.hook: That missed call looks like a $250 job — but a repair customer is worth five to ten grand over time. You're not losing a job, you're losing the relationship.

salon.pain: stylists serving clients can't reach the phone; 35-40% missed at peak
salon.hook: A new salon client looks like a $75 booking, but they're worth around $4,300 over the years they stay with you. Miss that first call and that whole relationship walks.

restaurant.pain: 30-40% of reservation calls missed during peak hours
restaurant.hook: Restaurants miss a third of calls at peak — every one is a $35 to $300 reservation or catering order going to the place that picked up.

vet.pain: emotional callers in urgent situations; after-hours gaps
vet.hook: A new vet client is worth three to eight hundred up front — and pet owners are emotional, urgent callers. If you don't answer, they panic-dial the next clinic.

dental_medical.pain: 45% of calls come outside 9-5; high lifetime patient value
dental_medical.hook: A new patient is worth anywhere from $850 to $7,500+ in lifetime value, and nearly half your calls come outside 9 to 5 — but because you handle health info, we set this up the compliant way with a signed agreement, never a shortcut.

generic.pain: 62% of small-business calls go unanswered; 85% never call back
generic.hook: Across small businesses, 62% of calls go unanswered and 85% of those people never call back — they just dial your competitor. That's the leak we plug.
